Q4 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • Expert • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

The question has 4 statements S1 to S4 followed by 4 conclusions in the form of options ‘a’ to ‘d’.
Identify the conclusion which logically follows from at least one of the 4 statements.
S1 : All senior schools are colleges.
S2 : No university is a play school.
S3 : Some primary schools are senior schools.
S4 : All colleges are universities.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Given that all senior schools are colleges and some primary schools are senior schools which leads to the conclusion that “all those primary schools which are senior schools are colleges” i.e. some of the colleges are primary schools.

Q10 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • Expert • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of these words most nearly means the word provided?
DEPLETE

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Deplete means to reduce or deprive or something essential; exhaust means to consume completely. Hence, 'Exhaust' is closest in meaning to 'Deplete'.

• Both sequential and direct access can be supported by contiguous allocation.

Q36 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
I. Deadlock freedom is necessary for satisfying bounded wait condition.
II. Deadlock freedom is necessary for satisfying progress.
Which of the above statement(s) is/are true?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Deadlock implies no progress, however deadlock has no relation with bounded waiting.
Therefore (b) is the answer.

Q59 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is true about 2 Phase Locking (2PL)?

Correct answer: A, B, C
Solution

(a, b, c)
2PL suffers from deadlock, starvation however 2PL ensures conflict serializability but does not guarantee recoverability.
∴ (a), (b), (c) is correct.
